name: New Synapse tool
description: Add a new declarative tool to this Synapse install (URL or process).
prompt:
I want to add a new tool to my local Synapse install. Synapse tools live under `tools/<id>/manifest.json` in the Synapse repo (not this scratch project). Walk me through it:

1. Ask me what the tool should do at a high level. Then suggest whether it should use the `url.open` tier (just opens a URL in the user's browser) or the `process.spawn` tier (launches a local command). Most tools start with `url.open`.
2. Pick a kebab-case id and create `tools/<id>/manifest.json` in the Synapse repo with the shape:
   {
     "id": "<id>",
     "name": "<Display name>",
     "description": "...",
     "actions": [{
       "id": "open",
       "name": "Open",
       "primitive": "url.open" | "process.spawn",
       "args": { ... }
     }]
   }
3. Hot-reload triggers automatically when the file lands; tell me what to look for in the Tools page of the dashboard to confirm it appeared.
4. Walk me through firing the action once, end-to-end.

Key environment variables exposed here: `SYNAPSE_API` for talking to the daemon, `SYNAPSE_TOKEN` for the auth header, `SYNAPSE_PROJECT_ID` for context. You can `curl $SYNAPSE_API/tools` with `-H "X-Synapse-Token: $SYNAPSE_TOKEN"` to inspect what's already there.
